subject: Gaming
title: LGBTQ+ Representation in Games
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on LGBTQ+ Representation in Games.

hook.text: Early games had zero LGBTQ+ characters. Today, major titles feature diverse identities as central to the story.

buildup.text: For decades, LGBTQ+ characters were either invisible or used as punchlines in mainstream games.

discovery.text: The Last of Us Part II featured a lesbian protagonist. It won Game of the Year and sold over 10 million.

twist.text: Some markets censor LGBTQ+ content in games. Global releases often face localization compromises.

climax.text: Indie games led representation first. Mainstream studios followed as audiences demanded authentic diversity.

punchline.text: From invisible to central. Representation in games changed a generation.

quiz.question: Which major game featured a lesbian protagonist and won Game of the Year?
options: The Last of Us Part II
